Let's face it everybody's got two cents and everybody's got two minutes So here's my two cents in under two minutes Hey Grant Cardone here, and I want to talk to you about how much the world is changing in this grant rat God tells me the other day grant nothing's changed everything's the same nothing really changes much Let me tell you how much America's changed just in the last 60 years in 1950 a gallon of gasoline cost 27 cents today It cost $3.69 that's an increase of 15 times in 1950 you could buy a first-class stamp for three cents in 2012 you'd wonder why you'd buy any kind of first-class stamp, but they're 45 cents if you do buy one in 1950 80% of all men in America had a job today at 65% or less in 1950 average duration of unemployment if you were unemployed, which was very low was 12 weeks today It's over 40 weeks the longest by the way in the history of the United States in 1950 the average family spent 22% of its income on housing in 2012 the average family will spend 43% of every dollar it makes on housing that's not owning a home. That's rent. That's a roof That's whether you own or rent 43% of every dollar you make and what's the point about this look the world is changing You have to change you have to change the way you operate the way you think the way you go into the marketplace You have to change and these facts that I'm giving you aren't even they're not even Inventions or improvements. I'm not talking about Google and and iPads and technology and clouds of technology I'm talking about just basic change Look, you got to change the way you're thinking you got to change the way you're performing in the marketplace You got to change the way you're acting jobs are being shipped overseas to China Furniture is not made in the United States cars in Japan are made in Mexico cars in the United States are made in Canada We're in a world economy to get competitive You got to change the way you think change the way you act change the way you operate and make sure you're around a group Of people that are willing to change and prepare and learn and study Look the marketplace is going to go to the people that evolve into the future It is not going to go to the people that are stuck in the past.
